Emergency Live

Emergency Care Layer

STABILIZE THE CARE FLOW

CarePath keeps AI triage, first aid guidance, facility matching, telemedicine, cost visibility, and follow-up in one controlled response view so the handoff from first report to treatment feels fast, calm, and connected.

Our Response

FIRST AID CONTROL

The full experience is built to reduce panic and cut repeat work. First responders, clinicians, and coordinators move in one chain, carrying the same patient context from the first note to facility referral, teleconsult, payment prep, and record capture.

View All Support Grid
Facility Matching Capabilities, distance, and wait time aligned
Cost Visibility M-Pesa ready estimates
Follow-Up Recovery and reminders remain tracked

Steps To Care

Move patients through one clean emergency pathway

01

Report Symptoms

Capture the first complaint through web, mobile, or lightweight access channels.

02

Log First Aid

Store the emergency details, responder notes, and stabilization guidance early.

03

Match Facility

Route by urgency, capability, travel time, and live care availability.

04

Connect Care

Launch telemedicine or in-person referral without losing the case context.

Next teleconsult 06
Matched centers 22
Failed handoffs 0
Care alerts 45

Response desk remains active across triage, referral, and follow-up care.

02 First Aid Hub

Capture the incident before handoff

03 Facility Matching

Match the patient to the best-fit care point

04 Telemedicine

Launch a remote consult with the right context

Frequently Asked Questions

Teams stay clear on what happens next.

The support layer keeps operators, facilities, and patients aligned on cost, bookings, follow-up, alerts, and records without splitting the case across disconnected tools.

Cost and Payments Appointments Follow-Up Low-Bandwidth Access Notifications EHR and Prescriptions
How does the first aid log stay attached to the patient case?

Every first response note stays in the same handoff trail used by triage, referrals, and telemedicine.

When do patients move to telemedicine instead of physical referral?

Stable non-emergency cases can be routed to chat, audio, or video consultation before travel is recommended.

Can facility matching account for wait time and distance together?

Yes. Matching considers urgency, distance, capability, and current availability to reduce unnecessary movement.

Does the platform still work for low-connectivity regions?

Web, mobile, USSD, and SMS flows all feed the same care record so the pathway stays consistent across access levels.

CAREPATH FIRST RESPONSE

Healthcare response coordination for triage, first aid, facility matching, telemedicine, and connected follow-up.
